warning.warningAndCautionPara: Dangerous conditions can occur if Critical Safety Items are damaged. Make sure Critical Safety Items are held and moved carefully. Failure to comply can result in loss of engine control, un-commanded engine shut down, or injury or death to personnel.
Component has sharp edges. Handle using extreme care. Failure to comply may result in injury to personnel.

caution.warningAndCautionPara: Scratches on rotating hardware can cause cracking and result in hardware failure. Do not scratch rotating hardware during maintenance. Failure to comply can result in damage to aircraft or equipment.

Component exterior/interior surfaces or surrounding structure are constructed of fragile material. Use care not to gouge, scratch, bend or damage during maintenance. Failure to comply may result in damage to equipment.

note.notePara: Task lists inspection criteria for 5th stage axial compressor rotor. It is not required for part to be removed to inspect areas that cannot be seen if part is installed.
There are 54 blades in 5th stage axial compressor rotor blade set.
Unless otherwise specified, any number of defects permitted for each inspection area, all quantities length, width, and depth permitted. Damage must be clearly separated.

Maximum of 16 airfoils can be blended. Maximum of two blends for each airfoil. Blends less than 0.003 inch depth do not count against total blend limits by blade or stage but still apply towards spacing and geometric requirements, with exception of blends on airfoil convex/concave surfaces deeper than 0.003 inch do count towards airfoil and stage blend limits.
Parent material of 5th stage axial compressor rotor is PWA 1227 titanium alloy.

para.#text: Visually inspect 5th stage axial compressor rotor (5th stage rotor) [1]. See , , , , , , , , , , , , , and .

segments.s1: Visually inspect 5th stage axial compressor rotor (5th stage rotor) [1]

entry.para: Condition/Type
Definition
Crack is narrow separation of material without fracture into two or more pieces, frequently located at leading edge of existing damage.

segments.s1: Crack is narrow separation of material without fracture into two or more pieces, frequently located at leading edge of existing damage

entry.para: Small, smoothly rounded hollow in surface.

segments.s1: Small, smoothly rounded hollow in surface

para.#text: Nicks are result of impact with hard body, and typically present with loss of material or hanging material. Nick, on airfoil zones, with sharp, zero-radius bottom is not serviceable and must be treated as tear.

segments.s1: Nicks are result of impact with hard body, and typically present with loss of material or hanging material
segments.s2: Nick, on airfoil zones, with sharp, zero-radius bottom is not serviceable and must be treated as tear

entry.para: V-notch is nick with well-defined and clearly visible bottom radius. V-notch nicks are permitted if clearly visible and discernible bottom radius can be seen.

segments.s1: V-notch is nick with well-defined and clearly visible bottom radius
segments.s2: V-notch nicks are permitted if clearly visible and discernible bottom radius can be seen

entry.para: Nicks can have roughed and raised material edge and are serviceable as-is if within depth limits.

segments.s1: Nicks can have roughed and raised material edge and are serviceable as-is if within depth limits

entry.para: Crack-like physical separation of material with no clearly visible or discernable defect bottom radius. Occasionally, deformed material can mask ability to clearly visualize defect bottom. In these cases it is assumed that there is tear.

segments.s1: Crack-like physical separation of material with no clearly visible or discernable defect bottom radius
segments.s2: Occasionally, deformed material can mask ability to clearly visualize defect bottom
segments.s3: In these cases it is assumed that there is tear

entry.para: Impact damage that results in deformed edges above and below impact location. Bottom of tear is sharp and crack-like.

segments.s1: Impact damage that results in deformed edges above and below impact location
segments.s2: Bottom of tear is sharp and crack-like

entry.para: Equivalent to two-sided tear, with deformation which occurs on only one of upper or lower edges. Bottom of tear is sharp and crack-like.

segments.s1: Equivalent to two-sided tear, with deformation which occurs on only one of upper or lower edges
segments.s2: Bottom of tear is sharp and crack-like

entry.para: Type of one-sided tear in which adjacent deformed material is folded in plane approximately parallel to airfoil edge. Deformed material masks defect bottom. Crack-like bottom might or might not be seen.

segments.s1: Type of one-sided tear in which adjacent deformed material is folded in plane approximately parallel to airfoil edge
segments.s3: Crack-like bottom might or might not be seen

para.#text: One-sided/folded tear with adjacent nick. See .

entry.para: Equivalent to one-sided tear or folded tear, but there is adjacent liberated material. Deformed material masks defect bottom. crack-like bottom might or might not be seen. This condition must not be confused with nick with hanging material.

segments.s1: Equivalent to one-sided tear or folded tear, but there is adjacent liberated material
segments.s3: crack-like bottom might or might not be seen
segments.s4: This condition must not be confused with nick with hanging material

entry.para: Soft body tears can occur in middle of curled damage location or to one side or other. If tear occurs in middle, damage will look like two-sided tear. If tear occurs to one side it will look like one-sided tear. Bottom of tear is sharp and crack-like.

segments.s1: Soft body tears can occur in middle of curled damage location or to one side or other
segments.s2: If tear occurs in middle, damage will look like two-sided tear
segments.s3: If tear occurs to one side it will look like one-sided tear
segments.s4: Bottom of tear is sharp and crack-like

entry.para: Nick with sharp, crack-like, zero-radius bottom.

segments.s1: Nick with sharp, crack-like, zero-radius bottom

entry.para: Hanging material can result when nicked material does not liberate. Nicks related to hanging material must have visible bottom and should not be confused with tears. Out-of-plane, deformed, and hanging material must be isolated to nicked damage. Material separation must be out-of-plane with airfoil and must not extend into airfoil. If bottom of nick cannot be seen, damage is considered tear. Hanging material is serviceable as-is and removal is not required. Post-blend fluorescent penetrant inspection is not required.

segments.s1: Hanging material can result when nicked material does not liberate
segments.s2: Nicks related to hanging material must have visible bottom and should not be confused with tears
segments.s3: Out-of-plane, deformed, and hanging material must be isolated to nicked damage
segments.s4: Material separation must be out-of-plane with airfoil and must not extend into airfoil
segments.s5: If bottom of nick cannot be seen, damage is considered tear
segments.s6: Hanging material is serviceable as-is and removal is not required
segments.s7: Post-blend fluorescent penetrant inspection is not required

entry.para: Airfoil edge deformed circumferentially from its as-produced location as determined from immediately adjacent undisturbed edge. Out-of-plane deformation is not raised material.

segments.s1: Airfoil edge deformed circumferentially from its as-produced location as determined from immediately adjacent undisturbed edge
segments.s2: Out-of-plane deformation is not raised material

entry.para: Gas path deposits.
Gas path deposits are non-metallic or non-adhered metal-like material such as dirt, abradable material, etc. on engine hardware after operation.

segments.s1: Gas path deposits are non-metallic or non-adhered metal-like material such as dirt, abradable material, etc

entry.para: Common chloride-type salts on engine hardware after operation.

segments.s1: Common chloride-type salts on engine hardware after operation

entry.para: Tip discoloration.

During normal engine operation tips of integrally bladed rotor (IBR) airfoils can become slightly discolored (multi-colored heat affected appearance). This condition is best seen on convex side of airfoil tip. Isolated local discoloration is permitted. More severe discoloration indicates airfoils have experienced abnormal tip rub (several airfoils together with discoloration found over majority of tip chord) and is not permitted.

segments.s1: During normal engine operation tips of integrally bladed rotor (IBR) airfoils can become slightly discolored (multi-colored heat affected appearance)
segments.s2: This condition is best seen on convex side of airfoil tip
segments.s4: More severe discoloration indicates airfoils have experienced abnormal tip rub (several airfoils together with discoloration found over majority of tip chord) and is not permitted

entry.para: Burned, rounded.

Burned airfoil tips are characterized by very ragged, usually rounded airfoil tips and traces of splattered abradable material adhering to airfoil surfaces. Abradable will also be gouged out, with splattered appearance.

segments.s1: Burned airfoil tips are characterized by very ragged, usually rounded airfoil tips and traces of splattered abradable material adhering to airfoil surfaces
segments.s2: Abradable will also be gouged out, with splattered appearance

entry.para: Curled tip.
Blade tip, leading or trailing edge, which is bent over, can have sharp edge.

segments.s1: Blade tip, leading or trailing edge, which is bent over, can have sharp edge

Maximum Serviceable Limit
Maximum Repairable Limit

para.#text: 12. AIRFOIL LEADING EDGE (AREA A2) - Nicks, dents. See , , , , , and .

segments.s1: 12. AIRFOIL LEADING EDGE (AREA A2) - Nicks, dents

para.#text: Two maximum combined between AREA A2, and AREA A3, 0.015 inch maximum depth in chordwise direction for damage contacting leading edge (extending into both concave and convex sides). Minimum 0.250 inch separation. See  and GROUP OR eight maximum, 0.010 inch maximum depth in chordwise direction for damage contacting leading edge (extending into both concave and convex sides). Minimum separation must be more than depth of deepest flaw present. Nicks and dents 0.002 inch round bottom and below must not be counted in overall defect count and separation limit does not apply as long as defects are visually separated. See  and GROUP OR four maximum nicks and dents for each airfoil combined across AREA A2, AREA A3, AREA B2, AREA B3, AREA G1, AREA G2, and AREA H. Include AREA E3 only for nicks and dents reported on those areas that are more than 0.004 inch up to 0.005 inch, 20 maximum surface nicks and dents combined across 10 airfoils, 0.005 inch maximum depth round bottom. Sharp bottom nicks are not serviceable. Minimum separation must be more than depth of deepest flaw present. Nicks and dents 0.002 inch round bottom and below must not be counted in overall defect count and separation limit does not apply as long as defects are visually separated. See  and .

para.#text: 13. AIRFOIL LEADING EDGE (AREA A2) - Scratches. See , , and .

para: Four maximum scratches for each airfoil, 20 maximum scratches combined across 10 airfoils, scratches cannot cross, 0.002 inch maximum depth, 0.170 inch maximum length GROUP OR 0.001 inch maximum depth. For scratches more than 0.001 inch depth that are separated by less than 0.015 inch length, those scratches must be considered as single scratch by adding their lengths.
